# paper-opinion-talk-is-cheap-2605-01750

The paper identifies 4 failure modes in LLM negotiation dyads: missing interaction history, stubborn anchoring, perfunctory fairness, and referential binding loss—using a rigorous 3-baseline methodology (oracle/no-talk/full-transparency) that distinguishes informational from structural coordination failures. Its core insight is that communication is necessary but insufficient; anchoring and referential binding require structural solutions beyond improved information sharing. The 3-baseline decomposition serves as a reusable diagnostic template for debugging multi-agent coordination failures.
